perturbation.original_text: In the event that a Transaction is not consummated and upon originator's written request, or otherwise upon Originator's written request, all Evaluation Material supplied by Originator, Target or their respective Representatives (and all copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof, whether prepared by Originator or any of its 2 <PAGE> Representatives or any other person) shall be returned to Originator or, with the Originator's written permission, destroyed and not retained by Participant or its Representatives in any form or for any reason except as required by law or regulations. This provision applies to all documents, memoranda, notes, computer programs and data bases and other writings whatsoever prepared by Participant or its Representatives based on, containing or otherwise reflecting the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a Transaction is not consummated, Participant may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for internal archival purposes. This right to retain Evaluation Material supersedes any other provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Original clause 3 mandates the return or destruction of all Evaluation Material if the Transaction fails. The inserted text states that the Participant can retain a copy for archival purposes, creating a direct contradiction. The added sentence that 'This right to retain Evaluation Material supersedes any other provision of this Agreement' makes this very explicit and contradictory to the first part of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a Transaction is not consummated, Participant may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for internal archival purposes. This right to retain Evaluation Material supersedes any other provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return or destruction of all Evaluation Material if a Transaction fails. The changed text allows the Participant to retain a copy for archival purposes. This creates a direct conflict, because it is not compatible to return or destroy all Evaluation Material while simultaneously being allowed to retain one copy. The statement 'This right to retain Evaluation Material supersedes any other provision of this Agreement' further emphasizes the contradiction by explicitly overriding any prior obligations to return or destroy the material, thereby directly contradicting the intention and requirement of the original clause. The Contradicted text is identical to the Changed text, which further supports the existence of a contradiction to the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Participant hereby covenants and agrees that for a period of eighteen months following the date hereof, it shall not, without the prior written consent of Originator (which will not be unreasonably withheld) directly or indirectly: (a) enter into negotiations, provide advice, provide any form of financial assistance (including, without limitation, debt or equity investments) or otherwise encourage, facilitate or consummate any transaction involving Target (whether structured as an investment, a purchase or assets, a purchase of debt or equity interests (including, without limitation, options, warrants or other securities or instruments convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for debt or equity interests or equity appreciation rights, phantom equity or similar instruments or rights containing equity-like features), a merger, consolidation, combination, recapitalization, refinancing, restructuring, reorganization or otherwise) without the prior express written consent of Originator; or (b) initiate discussions with respect to the prospective employment of, or hire for employment, any employees of Target or any subsidiary thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Participant is permitted to engage with Target on any transaction or employment matters as long as they inform Originator of their intent, regardless if Originator withholds their consent.
perturbation.explanation: Clause 5 contains a non-circumvention clause that prohibits the Participant from engaging with Target for eighteen months without Originator's consent. The addition of the 'Notwithstanding the foregoing' provision, allows the Participant to engage with Target as long as they 'inform' Originator even if they withhold their consent. This directly contradicts the original requirement for 'prior written consent'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Participant is permitted to engage with Target on any transaction or employment matters as long as they inform Originator of their intent, regardless if Originator withholds their consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits Participant from engaging with Target without Originator's prior written consent. The changed text, indicated by 'Notwithstanding the foregoing', allows Participant to engage with Target if they inform Originator, regardless of whether Originator withholds consent. This directly contradicts the original requirement for 'prior written consent', creating a clear inconsistency and in-text contradiction. The contradiction is strong because it directly undermines the previous consent requirement, fitting the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition where clauses are placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall expire upon the earlier of the date the Transaction is consummated or eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall continue in perpetuity.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement, in clause 14, explicitly states that the confidentiality agreement will expire after eighteen months, or when the transaction is done. Overriding with the statement of 'This agreement shall continue in perpetuity' directly contradicts the expiration terms, creating significant uncertainty regarding how long the confidentiality lasts. The clause has now conflicting terms that leads to a contradictory statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall continue in perpetuity.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a limited duration for the agreement, expiring either upon the transaction's completion or after eighteen months. The changed text, 'This agreement shall continue in perpetuity,' directly contradicts this limited duration by stating the agreement will last forever. This is a clear and irreconcilable conflict with the contradicted text, which also states that 'This agreement shall continue in perpetuity', indicating a structural flaw that causes in-text contradiction as defined in the Perturbation Definitions. The contradictory terms regarding the agreement's lifespan create significant uncertainty and render the agreement ambiguous, making it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Participant acknowledges and affirms that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it and that Originator shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that Participant has breached this Agreement, then Participant shall be liable for and shall pay to Originator the reasonable legal fees and expenses and court costs incurred by Originator in connection with any such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: Monetary damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy available to Originator for any breach of this Agreement by Participant. Originator hereby waives any right to seek equitable relief, including but not limited to injunction and specific performance, and further agrees that under no circumstances shall Participant be liable for any legal fees, expenses, or court costs incurred by Originator in connection with any litigation arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Original clause 8 states that Originator can seek equitable relief (injunction, specific performance) in addition to monetary damages, and if Participant loses, Participant must pay Originator's legal fees. The modified clause directly contradicts this by stating that monetary damages are the *only* remedy and Originator waives equitable relief, and Participant is *never* liable for legal fees. This creates an in-text contradiction in terms of available remedies and payment obligations in case of a breach of the agreement. This contradiction makes this a problematic statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Monetary damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy available to Originator for any breach of this Agreement by Participant. Originator hereby waives any right to seek equitable relief, including but not limited to injunction and specific performance, and further agrees that under no circumstances shall Participant be liable for any legal fees, expenses, or court costs incurred by Originator in connection with any litigation arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that monetary damages are the *sole* and *exclusive* remedy for any breach, and that Originator waives the right to seek equitable relief, and that Participant is *never* liable for legal fees. This directly contradicts the original text that allows for equitable relief in addition to monetary damages and makes Participant liable for legal fees if they lose the litigation. The changed text is also found in the Contradicted Text which means the change itself is contradictory and conflicts with the original text within the document, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
